title: Bashar Quote - We want to live in a Republic not a Democracy

transcript.text: Hello, this video is Bashar Quote. I'm going to try to, from now on, try to interpret Bashar Quotes.
Because I'm totally into Bashar, because every time I listen to Bashar, I feel like I'm talking directly to an alien, and I love that.
Now, one Bashar Quote is, we don't want to live in a democracy, we want to live in a republic.
Because if we live in a democracy, because we're all primitive people, we will all collectively decide that we're going to disenfranchise certain people,
Because the collective consciousness of a not very advanced civilization of people will, by default, decide to not respect, say, gay people.
You know, like, you know, like, like, you know, in the 60s, how we wouldn't let black people drink out of white people water fountains or sit in the front of the bus.
Some people might say that was a, you know, that that's not the collective decision of the people.
But in fact, it was, because, you know, every time, you know, bus driver is just a normal person,
Yet the bus driver enforces the law of not letting the black person sit in the front of the bus.
The person who works at the barbershop is actually, if he enforces and doesn't let the black person drink out of the water fountain,
Then he, then, you know, he may tell you that, oh, I don't believe it, but, you know, that's the way it is, so that's the way it is.
Well, that's the, that's a stupid excuse.
He does believe it if he's going to support it.
You know, you say, oh, well, you know, they'll get killed, that, that white barber will get killed by his friends
If he allows a black person to drink out of the white person water fountain.
Well, then his friends are upholding that idea.
And so, you know, you can't, you can't hide behind peer pressure.
You know, maybe on your conscious level, you, you, you're, you're afraid of your peers,
But on a subconscious level, at the end of the day, you have to take responsibility for your own actions.
And so, basically, if we're living in a primitive society, we don't want, we don't want a democracy
Because a democracy will just, will just all collectively decide to oppress people and make the wrong decisions.
And so, what we want is a republic, which is a democracy, which is something that,
And in the way that you, you all, you know, vote to decide on the laws and vote to decide on the leaders.
But, along with that, you also have to have a very clear constitution that is a mission statement
That makes people crystallize their highest desires so that, you know, you don't have to worry about peer pressure.
You can anonymously vote on making a resolution to allow black people to drink out of a white person water fountain.
And, you know, if you were to allow people, you know, if you were to allow people to anonymously vote to say,
Yes, you can do that, or to, you know, to, or have at least the smart leaders of society write, write those laws into, into being,
And then, and then instead of, um, instead of letting this, you know, the less intelligent people of society,
Uh, the more ignorant people of society, um, who are the masses, take over and, um, and oppress, uh, the minorities,
You would have the smarter people write down a constitution of good laws, um, to, uh, to respect them.
And then, and then, so then the people can go and, uh, follow along with that and, uh, and, um, and respect the minority.
So it's kind of like having a mission statement to help uphold your, uh, your highest ideals.
Because, you know, some people might say, oh, but if, but what if you have people writing into laws that are wrong?
Well, you know, oppressive laws, like make it a law that, that you can't, um, that you can't, uh, allow black people to drink out of the white person water fountain.
Well, um, that, um, well, that's how it was.
Um, so, but that, that, that wasn't the, that wasn't the, uh, that wasn't the, uh, representation of our highest, our highest, um, desires.
So I guess with the Republic, it would be the formate, the writing into laws, uh, the highest, uh, mind state of, uh, the best leaders of the country.
Anyways, that's my, uh, best interpretation of, um, translating what I was trying to say when he said we want a Republic and not a democracy.
So, what did I miss? Throw me a bone here, help me out.
I'm laughing at you.
